decisions.rationale: Private browser-profile maintenance belongs in a personal-operations capability system, not Stack.
Purchasing research is useful but outside Stack's design/build identity.
This operates the personal knowledge substrate rather than designing or building software.
Zettelkasten production and quota operations belong in the knowledge-operations system.
The capability is tied to private household finance and belongs in the family-operations system.
This produces Mookie graph taxonomy rather than a reusable design/build capability.
Personal storage and backup governance is outside Stack's product identity.
Vault source migration belongs in the knowledge-operations system.
This reviews personal knowledge-system migrations rather than product delivery.
Merge into the byte-identical top-level entrypoint currently consumed by Codex.
Keep as the canonical installed entrypoint.

Merge its useful checks and provenance into the canonical entrypoint that owns the supporting artifacts.

Keep the more complete version at the path currently consumed by Codex.
Merge provenance from the shorter overlapping suite version into the canonical CDO entrypoint.
It directly routes design/build requests through planning, implementation, review, and verification.
It supports model selection for bounded software-building work.
It directly orchestrates software-engineering execution.
